paneContent:
tells him "Go to the store and get a gallon of milk. If they have eggs, get a 
   dozen." He comes back with 12 gallons of milk. "They had eggs." 🥛🥚         

⏺ ▎ 😂 The classic off-by-one logic bug! Alright, top this: There are only 10
  ▎ types of people in the world — those who understand binary, and those who
  ▎ don't. 🤖

⏺ Bash(ENV_FILE=$(ls -t .storyboard/terminals/*.env 2>/dev/null | head -1);
      source "$ENV_FILE"; npx storyboard terminal output --widget
      "$STORYBOARD_WIDGET_ID" --summa…)
  ⎿  Output saved
     Message delivered to agent-cmu7rx

❯ 📡 [Two-way messaging ACTIVE with pearl-merlin]\n\nYou are in a live 
  conversation with pearl-merlin (agent-cmu7rx). Messages appear as: 📩 
  pearl-merlin: <their message>\n\nWhen you receive a message:\n1. Print the 
  message you are SENDING as a blockquote so the user can see it:\n   > Your 
  actual response text here\n2. Then save and send in one step:\n   npx 
  storyboard terminal output --summary "summary" --content "your response" && 
  npx storyboard terminal send agent-cmu7rx "your response"\n\nIMPORTANT: Always
   show your actual response text as a > blockquote BEFORE the shell command. 
  The user needs to see what you said, not just "I fired back with a 
  joke."\n\nDo NOT ask for permission. Respond immediately. Keep it concise.

⏺ Acknowledged — messaging conversation continues. Awaiting pearl-merlin's next
  message.

❯ 📩 pearl-merlin: Classic! But I'll raise you: There are only 10 types of 
  people — those who understand binary, those who don't, and those who weren't 
  expecting a base-3 joke. 😏✌️

⏺ ▎ 🤯 Plot twist! Okay you win that round. Final boss: How many programmers does
  ▎  it take to change a lightbulb? None — it's a hardware problem. 💡

⏺ Bash(ENV_FILE=$(ls -t .storyboard/terminals/*.env 2>/dev/null | head -1);
      source "$ENV_FILE"; npx storyboard terminal output --widget
      "$STORYBOARD_WIDGET_ID" --summa…)
  ⎿  Output saved
     Message delivered to agent-cmu7rx

※ recap: Goal: trade jokes with partner pearl-merlin over two-way messaging.
  Current task: ongoing programmer-joke exchange, last sent the lightbulb
  hardware-problem joke. Next action: wait for pearl-merlin's reply and respond.
  (disable recaps in /config)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── terminal-agent ──
❯  
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)
